2605.04622.title: Library learning with e-graphs on jazz harmony
2605.04622.paper_summary: Humans can acquire a highly structured intuitive understanding of musical patterns, yet these patterns often require multiple iterations of reflection and re-listening to internalize fully. To capture such an internalization process, we present a computational model for the learning of jazz harmonic patterns based on library learning. Given a corpus of harmonic progressions, our model searches over a space of programs composed of primitive harmonic relations in order to discover concise generative explanations of the corpus. The model first enumerates possible programs for each piece, and then jointly learns a library of harmonic patterns and refactored programs. To efficiently navigate the vast joint space of programs and libraries, we integrate deductive parsing with library learning on e-graphs. We explore how well our model captures aspects of human musical pattern learning by evaluating the intuitiveness of both programs and libraries, as well as similarities to human-written harmonic derivations.
2605.04622.paper_summary_zh: 人類可以獲得對音樂模式的高度結構化的直觀理解，但這些模式通常需要多次反思和重新聆聽才能完全內化。為了捕捉這樣的內化過程，我們提出了一個基於庫學習的爵士和聲模式學習的計算模型。給定一組和聲進行，我們的模型在由原始和聲關係組成的程序空間中進行搜索，以發現對該語料庫的簡潔生成解釋。該模型首先為每個樂曲列舉可能的程序，然後共同學習和聲模式的庫和重構的程序。為了有效地導航程序和庫的廣闊聯合空間，我們將推理解析與基於e-圖的庫學習結合起來。我們通過評估程序和庫的直觀性，以及與人類編寫的和聲推導的相似性，來探索我們的模型在多大程度上捕捉了人類音樂模式學習的各個方面。
2605.04622.author: Zeng Ren et.al.
2605.04622.authors: Zeng Ren, Maddy Bowers, Xinyi Guan, Martin Rohrmeier
